rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Do not introduce package-qualified names that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Use only existing component features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation-level assignments.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type errors, trace the root cause to an unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration and resolve it.
AGREE contracts must be placed in valid scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Avoid introducing new package dependencies that are not already present in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Guarantee expressions must have a boolean type; comparisons and arithmetic determine the type.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics; only repair existing constructs.
Do not reference identifiers that are not declared or visible in the current scope.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assignments and equations.
